title: AI transparency statement | Food Standards Australia New Zealand

sections.text:
Food Standards Australia New Zealand (FSANZ) recognises the potential for Artificial Intelligence (AI) to support innovation. This statement is part of our compliance with the Policy for the responsible use of AI in government, as adopted from 1 September 2024.

Please refer to the FSANZ Corporate Plan 2024-25 for our key activities that AI may impact over time.

The following table outlines how FSANZ currently complies with the key requirements of the policy.* More information about AI classification can be found at: AI Use Classification

sections.heading: Intentions behind FSANZ use of AI
sections.text: Improve customer service and workplace productivity by supporting staff.

sections.heading: Classification of AI*
sections.text: Usage patterns: Workplace productivity. Domains: Service delivery, Corporate and enabling.

sections.heading: Classification of use where the public may directly interact with, or be significantly impacted by, AI without a human intermediary or intervention
sections.text: No impact on the public without human intermediary or intervention.

sections.heading: Measures to monitor the effectiveness of deployed AI systems, such as governance or processes
sections.text:
FSANZ controls its usage to known systems providing limited services.

Should FSANZ expand usage outside of the above classifications, further governance will be implemented.

sections.heading: Compliance with applicable legislation and regulation
sections.text: FSANZ complies with related legislation and regulation in this regard.

sections.heading: Efforts to identify and protect the public against negative impacts
sections.text: Staff using supportive AI technologies will be trained to ensure they comply with the principles from the Australian Government generative AI guidance.

sections.heading: Compliance with each requirement under the Policy for responsible use of AI in government
sections.text: Enable and prepare: Accountable Officer: Chief Information Officer. Regular staff training: Available to staff. Understanding AI usage: Controlled through policy. Integration into other frameworks: Will occur during improvement cycles. Engage responsibly: Transparency statement: Applied (this document). Participation in AI assurance pilot: Under consideration. Issuance of generative AI guidance: This has been applied internally as guidance. Evolve and integrate: Monitoring and evaluation approaches: Under consideration. Participating in whole of government AI approaches: Considered on a case-by-case approach.
